description: Small retrieval eval for HealthCompanion. Builds an index from the documents below, runs each query through the real retrieval path (hybrid + MMR), and checks whether the expected terms appear in the retrieved chunks. Uses Gemini embeddings only (no vision).

documents.text:
Greenfield Family Clinic
Patient: Ravi Kumar  Age: 54  Date: 2026-05-14

Diagnosis:
Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, Hypertension, Dyslipidemia.

Medications:
1. Metformin 500 mg - 1 tablet twice daily after meals (morning and night) - 30 days
2. Amlodipine 5 mg - 1 tablet once daily in the morning - 30 days
3. Atorvastatin 10 mg - 1 tablet at night after dinner - 30 days
4. Vitamin D3 60000 IU - 1 capsule once weekly on Sunday - 8 weeks

Advice:
Reduce salt intake. Recheck blood sugar in 4 weeks.

Metro Diagnostics - Lab Report
Date: 2024-02-01

Lab Results:
HbA1c: 8.1 %
Fasting blood glucose: 156 mg/dL
LDL cholesterol: 142 mg/dL
Serum creatinine: 0.9 mg/dL

Impression: Suboptimal glycaemic control.

queries.q: which medicines should I take at night?
how do I take vitamin D?
what was the LDL cholesterol value?
what is my blood pressure reading?
